summary: Keyword co-occurrence in bundled output without traced installer-harm path
details: Pattern matches in the minified dist/index.js show co-occurrence of require("child_process"), an HTTP method token, and identifier-shaped keywords. None of the structural fingerprints of a supply-chain attack are present in the available evidence: no hardcoded attacker C2 endpoint bound to install-time or import-time code, no browser-credential or wallet-extension enumeration, no seed-phrase or token regex extraction, no known-bad dropper host, no lifecycle script in package.json invoking remote fetch+execute. Without a traced execution path the keyword co-occurrence in a single bundled file is a weak signal — child_process plus HTTP plus identifiers commonly appears in legitimate build/CLI tooling bundles. Routing for human review rather than blocking on pattern proximity alone.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
